description: To perceive through any of the body's channels—the umbrella act that includes seeing, hearing, touching, tasting, and smelling, as well as subtler perceptions like proprioception and intuition. 'Morae' names the fundamental capacity to receive information from world through body, the basis of all embodied knowing.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'mo', the nasal of mindful attention turning inward to notice what the body receives. The 'o' opens receptively. Then 'ra' flows outward as perception reaches toward its object. Finally 'e' lifts into awareness, sensation becoming conscious experience.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi themo morae' (I sense something). Can specify the sense channel: 'mi mirae nua morae' (I sense with eyes / I sense visually). Covers all sensory modalities as general term.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sensing is the first step in the chain of dependent origination—contact arises, feeling follows. Mindful sensing interrupts automatic reactivity.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional survival depended on keen sensing—reading weather, tracking animals, knowing plants. The body's wisdom begins in sensation.
